title: Climber Ground Fall — Granite Slab
briefing: 45-year-old climber took a 30-foot ground fall onto rock at the base of a granite slab. Briefly unresponsive, now responds to pain only. Obvious deformity at the right hip/thigh and pelvic pain. High-energy mechanism — assume spine and internal injury until cleared. Helicopter access is possible at this location.
terrain: Open granite slab with a viable helicopter landing/hoist site; ground carry-out would be long and rough
evacNote: High-mechanism blunt trauma — spine precautions, pelvic binder, immediate helicopter evac

patient.mechanism: 30 ft ground fall onto rock; high-energy blunt trauma

injuries.notes: Suspected unstable pelvis and right femur fracture. GCS unclear; responds to pain (AVPU=P). Spine precautions in place. Monitor for internal hemorrhage and shock.
